purpose: Maps an ERP/software system name (as a client describes it, colloquially, in an audit) to its optimal connection architecture - protocol, auth method, known constraints. Mirrors interconnected_systems_ontology.json's pattern (keyword-based, fuzzy-matchable), but for INTEGRATION knowledge rather than PROCESS knowledge.

connectors.name: SAP S/4HANA Cloud
connectors.rate_limit_class: moderate - SAP enforces tenant-level API call quotas
known_constraints: OData entity sets vary by SAP module activated - confirm which Business APIs are licensed before designing the integration
Cloud ALM / SAP API Business Hub is the source of truth for available endpoints per tenant
connectors.recommended_pattern: Direct REST/OData calls from the agent runtime; no middleware needed for most standard processes

connectors.name: SAP ECC / S/4HANA On-Premise
connectors.protocol: RFC/BAPI (via SAP NetWeaver Gateway or RFC SDK), IDoc for batch/async
connectors.auth_method: SAP user/role-based (RFC trusted connection or service user)
connectors.latency_class: moderate - depends on network path to the client's data center, RFC calls are synchronous
connectors.rate_limit_class: depends on SAP Basis-configured dialog/work process limits, not a typical cloud API quota
known_constraints: On-premise SAP almost always requires VPN or dedicated network connectivity - cannot be reached directly from a public-cloud-hosted agent without a tunnel or middleware
RFC/BAPI integration commonly goes through SAP PI/PO or SAP Cloud Connector as middleware, not direct from external code
Custom Z-transactions/Z-BAPIs are common in older ECC installs and must be confirmed with the client's SAP Basis team, not assumed from standard documentation
connectors.recommended_pattern: SAP Cloud Connector + middleware (PI/PO or a lightweight RFC gateway service) - rarely a direct connection

connectors.name: Salesforce
connectors.protocol: REST API (standard objects/records) + Bulk API (large data volumes)
connectors.auth_method: OAuth 2.0 (JWT bearer flow recommended for server-to-server)
connectors.rate_limit_class: API call limits scale with Salesforce edition/license - must be checked per org, not assumed
known_constraints: Governor limits apply per-transaction within Salesforce-side automation (Apex), separate from the external API call limits
connectors.recommended_pattern: Direct REST API calls; Bulk API specifically for high-volume batch operations

connectors.name: Microsoft Dynamics 365
connectors.auth_method: OAuth 2.0 via Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD)
connectors.rate_limit_class: Microsoft enforces per-tenant API request limits (varies by license tier)
connectors.recommended_pattern: Direct REST/OData calls against the Dataverse Web API

connectors.name: NetSuite
connectors.rate_limit_class: concurrency/governance limits per NetSuite account tier
connectors.recommended_pattern: REST-based SuiteTalk for new integrations; SOAP only if the client already has it in use

connectors.name: Workday
connectors.protocol: SOAP (Workday Web Services) and REST (newer endpoints)
connectors.rate_limit_class: not publicly standardized - confirm with client's Workday tenant configuration
known_constraints: Workday's API surface is large and module-specific - confirm exactly which Workday product (HCM, Financials, etc.) before assuming endpoint availability
connectors.recommended_pattern: REST where available, SOAP for legacy/complete coverage

connectors.name: Manual process (Excel/spreadsheets, email)
connectors.protocol: File-based (CSV/XLSX import-export) - no real-time API exists by definition
connectors.auth_method: N/A - file access only (shared drive, email attachment, SFTP)
known_constraints: This is not a software integration - it is the ABSENCE of one. Confirmed multiple times during today's audit testing: a client describing 'lo hacemos a mano con Excel' has no system to connect to yet.

The real deliverable here is usually a structured intake (form, lightweight upload, or a first system of record) BEFORE any agent automation - building an agent that reads an ad-hoc spreadsheet is fragile and should be flagged for the client as a precondition, not silently treated as equivalent to a real API integration.
connectors.recommended_pattern: Do not treat as equivalent to a system integration - flag missing_information in the SIOP (see functional_consultant.py's existing missing_information field) recommending a real intake mechanism first

known_constraints: This system was not found in the connector library. Do not assume a connection method - this must be researched against the specific client's version/deployment before any integration work begins.
fallback.recommended_pattern: Flag for human research before proceeding - same needs_human_review principle already used for synthesized organisms in functional_consultant.py
